intended_use: The Swoop Portable MR Imaging System is a portable, ultra-low field magnetic resonance imaging device for producing images that display the internal structure of the head where full diagnostic examina
intended_use_full: The Swoop Portable MR Imaging System is a portable, ultra-low field magnetic resonance imaging device for producing images that display the internal structure of the head where full diagnostic examination is not clinically practical. When interpreted by a trained physician, these images provide information that can be useful in determining a diagnosis.
target_condition: Various intracranial pathologies including stroke, tumor, traumatic brain injury, intracranial hemorrhage, and other head abnormalities
ground_truth: For reconstruction performance: synthetic k-space generated from reference images (Swoop data, high field images, and synthetic contrast images); for clinical validation: expert review by four American Board of Radiology (ABR)-certified radiologists
other_metrics: Normalized Mean Squared Error (NMSE) and Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) for reconstruction fidelity; Contrast-to-Noise Ratio (CNR) for pathology preservation; radiologist image quality ratings on 5-point Likert scale achieving median score of 2 (most positive) across noise, sharpness, contrast, geometric fidelity, artifact, and overall image quality categories
